name: The City of Archives
intro_text: Your mind has been… displaced. You wake in a cone-shaped body, your consciousness transferred into a member of the Great Race of Yith in their subterranean city of Pnakotus.

The Great Race is methodical and ancient beyond reckoning. They have brought you here to extract information from your era — but they have also taken something from you. You must recover your stolen memories and find a way back to your own body.
Navigate the impossible geometry of Pnakotus, avoid the flying polyps that haunt its depths, and reach the Deconstruction Room before your identity is erased entirely.
setup_instructions: Each investigator's physical trauma is set aside — in this scenario, investigators cannot gain physical trauma (they have no physical body).
Place the Interview Room (start revealed). All investigators begin there, having just 'arrived' in Yithian bodies.
Investigators draw 2 fewer cards at the start of the game — their memories are fragmented.
The Great Race of Yith are not enemies by default; they become hostile when investigators act against the archive's rules.
Advance Act 1 when investigators gather enough clues to navigate Pnakotus. The Deconstruction Room is revealed only in Act 3.

resolutions.R1: You recover your memories and find the resonance device to return to your body. Record in the campaign log: *The investigators escaped from Pnakotus intact.* Each investigator earns 3 experience.
resolutions.R2: You escape but with fragmented memories. Record: *The investigators escaped but lost some memories.* Each investigator earns 2 experience but suffers 1 mental trauma.
resolutions.defeat: The Great Race repossesses your mind. Each investigator suffers 2 mental trauma and is eliminated.
